customRecipes.strInstructions:
STEP 1: Cut 500g chicken into medium pieces. In a large bowl, mix with 1 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, salt to taste, and 2 tbsp lemon juice. Marinate for 30 minutes.

STEP 2: In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat 4 tbsp ghee. Add 4 black cardamom, 3 bay leaves, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 4 cloves, and 6 black peppercorns. Sauté for 1 minute.

STEP 3: Add marinated chicken and cook on high heat for 5 minutes until it starts browning. Remove and keep aside.

STEP 4: In a separate large bowl, parboil 2 cups basmati rice in boiling water with 1 tsp salt for 5 minutes until 70% cooked. Drain well.

STEP 5: In the same ghee pot, layer half the parboiled rice, then the cooked chicken, then remaining rice on top.

STEP 6: Dissolve a pinch of saffron in 1 cup warm milk and pour over the rice. Sprinkle chopped mint and cilantro on top.

STEP 7: Cover the pot with aluminum foil, place a tawa (cast iron griddle) on top, then close with the lid. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes until steam forms.

STEP 8: Lower heat to medium and dum cook for 45 minutes. Do not open the lid. The biryani is done when you hear a gentle sizzle and aroma releases.

STEP 1: For potato masala filling: Boil 3 medium potatoes until tender, peel and cut into cubes.

STEP 2: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan, add 1 tsp mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds, followed by 1 chopped onion and 2 green chilies, sauté until golden.

STEP 3: Add the potato cubes, 1/4 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, salt to taste, and 2 tbsp chopped cilantro. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. Set aside.

STEP 4: Prepare dosa batter (use store-bought or make from rice flour and urad flour). The batter should be thin and spreadable.

STEP 5: Heat a non-stick tawa or crepe pan on medium-high heat. Lightly oil it and pour 1/2 cup dosa batter in the center.

STEP 6: Using the back of a ladle, quickly spread the batter in a circular motion from center outward to make a thin crepe, about 8-9 inches diameter.

STEP 7: Drizzle 1/2 tsp oil around the edges and cook for 1-2 minutes until the bottom turns golden and crispy.

STEP 8: Spoon 3-4 tbsp hot potato masala along the center of the dosa.

STEP 9: Fold the dosa in half or into a triangle, ensuring the masala is encased. Transfer to a plate.

STEP 10: Serve hot with coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and sambar. Repeat for remaining dosa.

STEP 1: Cut 700g chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl, combine with 1/2 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1 tbsp lemon juice. Marinate for 30 minutes.

STEP 2: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a skillet. Cook marinated chicken on high heat until no longer pink outside (about 8-10 minutes). Remove and set aside.

STEP 3: In the same skillet, add 3 tbsp butter and heat until foaming. Add 1 tbsp minced garlic and 1 tbsp minced ginger, sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 4: Add 1 large diced onion and cook until translucent, about 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

STEP 5: Add 2 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to caramelize. Then add 1 cup crushed tomatoes and stir well.

STEP 6: Add 1.5 tsp garam masala powder, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, 1/2 tsp coriander powder, and 1/4 tsp red chili powder. Cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat, stirring constantly.

STEP 7: Return the cooked chicken to the pan and mix well with the sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes.

STEP 8: Stir in 1 cup heavy cream, 2 tbsp butter, salt to taste, and 1/2 tsp sugar. Simmer for 3-4 minutes until the gravy reaches desired consistency.

STEP 9: Garnish with 2 tbsp fresh cilantro and 1 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves). Serve hot with basmati rice or naan.

STEP 1: Soak 2 cups dried chickpeas overnight in water. Drain and cook in a pressure cooker with 4 cups water, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp baking soda for 5-6 whistles until completely soft.

STEP 2: For the masala base: Heat 3 tbsp oil in a large pot. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds, 2 bay leaves, 4 dried red chilies, and 1 inch ginger piece (crushed). Let the spices crackle for 1 minute.

STEP 3: Add 2 large diced onions and cook until golden brown, stirring frequently (about 8 minutes). Add 3 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

STEP 4: Add 1 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 1 minute. Then add 1.5 cups tomato puree and cook for 3-4 minutes, allowing the oil to separate.

STEP 5: Add cooked chickpeas (including 1 cup cooking liquid), 2 tsp cumin powder, 2 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp kasuri methi, and salt to taste. Simmer for 10-15 minutes on low heat until the gravy thickens.

STEP 6: For bhature dough: Mix 2 cups all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup yogurt, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp baking soda, 1 tbsp oil, and warm water to form a soft dough. Let it rest for 4-6 hours.

STEP 7: Divide dough into 8 equal parts. Roll each into a thin disc, approximately 6-7 inches diameter.

STEP 8: Heat oil for deep frying in a large and deep pan to 350°F (175°C). Carefully place one bhature disc into the hot oil.

STEP 9: The bhature will sink initially. As it fries, it will puff up beautifully. Fry for 1-2 minutes on each side until golden brown.

STEP 10: Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve hot chole masala alongside bhaturegarnis with sliced onion, pickle, and green chutney.

STEP 1: Cut 500g paneer into 1-inch cubes. In a bowl, combine 1/2 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1/4 tsp salt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and 1/2 tsp garam masala. Mix paneer cubes in this marinade and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

STEP 2: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a cast iron skillet or grill pan until smoking. Remove marinated paneer from yogurt (reserving excess marinade) and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until golden-brown grill marks appear. Remove and set aside.

STEP 3: Soak 1/2 cup cashew pieces in warm water for 15 minutes. Drain and grind with 1/4 cup water to a smooth paste.

STEP 4: In the same pan, add 3 tbsp butter and heat until foaming. Add 1 tbsp minced ginger and 1 tbsp minced garlic, sauté for 1 minute until golden.

STEP 5: Add 1 large finely diced onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. Add 2 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.

STEP 6: Add 1 cup crushed tomatoes and cook on high heat for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and oil separates from the sauce.

STEP 7: Reduce heat to low and stir in the cashew paste, 1 cup heavy cream, reserved paneer marinade, 1.5 tsp garam masala powder, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, 1/4 tsp chili powder, salt to taste, and 1/2 tsp sugar. Simmer for 5 minutes.

STEP 8: Gently return the grilled paneer pieces to the sauce and simmer for 3-4 minutes to infuse flavors.

STEP 9: Turn off heat. Garnish with 2 tbsp fresh cilantro and 1 tbsp kasuri methi. Finish with a dollop of cream if desired.

STEP 10: Serve hot with basmati rice, naan, or kulcha. Accompany with sliced onion and lemon wedges.

STEP 1: Soak 2 cups basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Cut 800g mutton/lamb into medium pieces.

STEP 2: Make the marinade: Mix 1.5 cups yogurt, 3 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 2 tsp red chili powder, 1.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and salt. Marinate mutton for 1 hour (or overnight for better flavor).

STEP 3: In a heavy-bottomed biryani pot (handi or thick-bottomed vessel), heat 6 tbsp ghee on high heat. Add 6 black cardamom, 4 black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 6 cloves, 8 black peppercorns, 8 bay leaves, and 2 star anise. Let the spices crackle for 2 minutes.

STEP 4: Add the marinated mutton and cook on high heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mutton starts browning and the yogurt is absorbed.

STEP 5: Boil water in a large pot with 1 tbsp salt. Add soaked rice and cook until 60% done (grains should still have a slight bite). Do not fully cook. Drain immediately.

STEP 6: Layer the partially cooked meat (with all the ghee and spices) at the bottom of the biryani pot. Layer half the parboiled rice on top of the meat.

STEP 7: Soak a pinch of saffron in 1 cup warm milk with 1/4 tsp rose water. Pour half this milk mixture over the rice. Sprinkle 1 tbsp chopped mint and 1 tbsp chopped cilantro.

STEP 8: Add the remaining partially cooked rice, then the remaining saffron milk, and garnish with fried onions (2 tbsp), mint, and cilantro.

STEP 9: Dot the top with 2 tbsp ghee. Cover the pot with aluminum foil, then place a tawa on top, and finally cover with the lid.

STEP 10: Place the pot on high heat for 3-4 minutes until you hear the sizzle and steam forms. Then place the entire pot on a tawa on low heat and cook for 45 minutes. Do not open the lid.

STEP 11: After 45 minutes, turn off the heat and wait for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and gently open the lid. Fluff the biryani with a fork.

STEP 1: Soak 2 cups parboiled rice in water for 30 minutes. Simultaneously, soak 1 cup urad dal (split skinned black lentils) and 1/4 cup fenugreek seeds for 30 minutes.

STEP 2: Drain the rice thoroughly. Using a grinding stone or modern mixer, grind the rice with minimal water to a thick, slightly coarse, yet smooth paste (takes about 20-30 minutes on traditional stone).

STEP 3: Drain the urad dal and fenugreek seeds. Grind them with 1/2 cup water into a light, fluffy, whipped batter. This takes 15-20 minutes on a traditional stone and is crucial for airy idlis.

STEP 4: Combine the ground rice and urad dal batters in a large bowl. Add 2 tsp salt and mix well. Do not beat the batter after mixing; gentle folding is key.

STEP 5: Cover the batter and let it ferment at room temperature for 8-12 hours (longer fermentation in cold climates). The batter should rise and develop a slightly sour, pleasant aroma when fermented.

STEP 6: Heat water in a large idli cooker or steamer pot. Place the metal stand inside.

STEP 7: Grease idli molds with oil or ghee. Fill each mold cavity two-thirds full with batter (do not overfill). Place filled molds on the stand.

STEP 8: Cover with a lid and steam for 8-10 minutes. The idlis are done when a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean and the top is no longer wet to touch.

STEP 9: Remove the mold from steam. Let it cool for 1-2 minutes. Using a blunt tool, gently push the idlis out of the molds onto a plate.

STEP 10: Serve hot with sambar, coconut chutney, and tomato chutney. Idlis are soft and fluffy when served immediately.

STEP 1: Soak 1 cup toor dal in water for 30 minutes. Chop the following vegetables into bite-sized pieces: 1 medium carrot, 1 medium potato, 1 small brinjal (eggplant), 2 ladies finger (okra), and 8-10 drumstick pieces.

STEP 2: In a small pan, dry-roast sambar powder ingredients: 3 tbsp coriander seeds, 1.5 tbsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp black peppercorn, 4-5 dried red chilies, 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds, and 1/4 tsp asafoetida powder. Toast on low heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant. Cool and grind to a fine powder. (Or use 3 tbsp store-bought sambar powder).

STEP 3: In a large pot, boil 3 cups water. Add the soaked toor dal and cook until completely soft (about 15-20 minutes). Mash the dal slightly using the back of a spoon.

STEP 4: To the cooked dal, add all the chopped vegetables and tamarind paste (4 tbsp), turmeric powder (1/2 tsp), and salt to taste. Bring to a boil and cook until vegetables are tender, about 15-20 minutes.

STEP 5: Add the roasted sambar powder (or store-bought sambar powder) and stir well. Simmer for 2 minutes. The sambar should have a tangy and slightly thick consistency.

STEP 6: Prepare tadka (tempering): Heat 2 tbsp oil or ghee in a small pan. Add 1 tsp mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds, 2-3 curry leaves, 2 dried red chilies, and 1/4 tsp asafoetida powder. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 7: Pour the hot tempering over the sambar and stir in. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

STEP 8: Sambar is ready to serve hot with idli, dosa, uttapam, or rice.

STEP 1: Cut 600g boneless, skinless chicken breasts into 1.5-inch cubes. In a large bowl, combine 3/4 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp ginger (minced), 3 green chilies (finely chopped), 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and 1/2 tsp salt.

STEP 2: Marinate the chicken pieces in this mixture for at least 2 hours (overnight is ideal). This enhances tenderness and flavor absorption.

STEP 3: Thread marinated chicken onto metal or wooden skewers (if using wooden, soak in water for 30 minutes first). 

STEP 4: Heat a cast iron skillet or grill pan until very hot. Brush with oil. Place the skewers on the hot surface.

STEP 5: Cook for 3-4 minutes on each side until the chicken develops charred spots and is cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C). Remove from heat.

STEP 6: For the sauce, heat 3 tbsp butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add 4 cloves minced garlic and 1 tbsp minced ginger. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 7: Add 1 large finely diced onion and cook until soft and golden, about 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally.

STEP 8: Add 2 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, allowing it to caramelize slightly. Then add 1 cup crushed tomatoes.

STEP 9: Cook the tomato mixture on high heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and the oil separates from the sauce. You should see the ghee/oil pooling on the sides.

STEP 10: Reduce heat to low. Add 1 cup heavy cream, cream cheese (2 tbsp), and 1/2 cup water. Stir well to combine.

STEP 11: Add 1.5 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/4 tsp red chili powder, salt and pepper to taste, and 1/2 tsp sugar.

STEP 12: Simmer on low heat for 3-4 minutes until well combined. Remove the tikka chicken from skewers and add to the sauce.

STEP 13: Simmer for 4-5 minutes to allow the chicken to absorb the flavors. The gravy should be smooth, creamy, and of medium thickness.

STEP 14: Garnish generously with 3 tbsp fresh cilantro and 1 tbsp kasuri methi. Finish with a dot of cream or butter on top, if desired.

STEP 15: Serve hot with steamed basmati rice, garlic naan, or butter naan. Accompany with sliced onion and lemon wedges.

STEP 1: Trim 800g lamb or mutton into 2-inch cubes, removing excess fat. In a large bowl, marinate with 1 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and 1/2 tsp salt for 30 minutes.

STEP 2: Soak 1/2 cup Kashmiri chilies (whole, deseeded) in 1 cup warm water for 10 minutes. Blend to a smooth paste. Also soak a large pinch of saffron in 1/2 cup warm milk.

STEP 3: Heat 4 tbsp ghee in a heavy-bottomed Dutch oven or large pot on high heat. Working in batches to avoid overcrowding, brown the marinated lamb pieces on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per batch. Do not cook through; just develop color. Set aside all meat.

STEP 4: In the same ghee, add 6 green cardamom, 4 black cardamom, 1.5 inch cinnamon stick, 5 cloves, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, and 3 bay leaves. Let the spices crackle for 2 minutes.

STEP 5: Add 3 large finely sliced onions. Cook until deep golden brown, about 12-15 minutes, stirring frequently. This is crucial for Rogan Josh's depth of flavor.

STEP 6: Add 4 tbsp ginger (finely minced) and 6 cloves garlic (finely minced). Sauté for 2 minutes until very fragrant.

STEP 7: Add the Kashmiri chili paste and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen the color and flavor. The mixture should darken slightly.

STEP 8: Add 1.5 cups tomato puree and cook for 5 minutes on high heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the mixture.

STEP 9: Return the browned lamb, along with all its juices, to the pot. Add 2 tsp coriander powder, 1.5 tsp cumin powder, 1.5 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, salt to taste, and 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder for color.

STEP 10: Mix well and cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Then reduce heat to low, add 1 cup water and saffron milk (reserving some for garnish).

STEP 11: Cover the pot and simmer on low heat for 1.5-2 hours until the lamb is extremely tender. Meat should break apart easily with a spoon. Stir occasionally to ensure even cooking.

STEP 12: After 1.5 hours, check the consistency. The gravy should be thick and cling to the meat. If too thin, increase heat and simmer uncovered until it thickens.

STEP 13: Taste and adjust salt and spices. Add 1/2 tsp sugar for balance. Finish with 2 tbsp ghee and 2 tbsp fresh cilantro. Drizzle reserved saffron milk on top.

STEP 14: Serve hot, garnished with whole cilantro leaves, toasted almond slices, and boiled eggs if desired. Serve with steamed basmati rice, Indian bread (naan, roti), or potato preparations.

STEP 1: For the sugar syrup: Combine 2 cups sugar, 2.5 cups water, 4 green cardamom (crushed), 1/4 tsp saffron strands, 1/2 tsp rose water, 1 inch small piece of ginger (bruised), and 2 cloves in a large saucepan.

STEP 2: Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes. The syrup should coat the back of a spoon lightly. Set aside to cool to room temperature. (Important: The syrup must be warm or room temperature when gulab jamun are added, never hot.)

STEP 3: For gulab jamun dough: In a bowl, combine 1 cup milk powder (full-fat, fresh), 1/2 cup all-purpose flour, 1/4 cup semolina, 1/4 tsp baking soda, 1/4 tsp salt, 1 tsp cardamom powder, and a pinch of nutmeg powder.

STEP 4: Add 1 tbsp ghee and 2-3 tbsp whole milk to the dry mixture. Mix gently with your fingertips until the mixture resembles wet sand. Do not knead like regular dough; overmixing will result in hard gulab jamun.

STEP 5: Add more milk just 1 tsp at a time if the mixture is too dry. The dough should just hold together when pressed. It will be slightly moist and crumbly.

STEP 6: Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15 minutes. This resting allows the semolina to absorb the milk.

STEP 7: Divide the dough into 20-24 equal portions. Using the palms of your hands, roll each portion into a smooth, crack-free ball. The key is gentle handling; if cracks appear, the jamun will fall apart in the syrup. If dough becomes sticky, dust lightly with milk powder.

STEP 8: Heat oil for deep frying in a heavy-bottomed vessel or wok to 325°F (160°C). Use a thermometer for accuracy; temperature is crucial—too hot and the outside burns before inside cooks; too cool and they absorb excess oil.

STEP 9: Carefully slide 4-5 gulab jamun into warm oil. Fry on low heat for 6-7 minutes, stirring gently and frequently with a slotted spoon to ensure even browning from all sides. They should be golden-brown and sound slightly hollow when tapped.

STEP 10: Remove with a slotted spoon and immediately immerse in the warm sugar syrup. The syrup's temperature is critical—it causes the gulab jamun to absorb it and puff up. If the syrup is too hot, the outside hardens before the inside absorbs the syrup.

STEP 11: Let them soak in the syrup for at least 2-3 hours (or overnight) before serving. They can be eaten warm or chilled.

STEP 12: Serve standalone or with rabri (reduced sweetened milk) and garnish with pistachios and almonds if desired.

STEP 1: Wash 1 kg fresh spinach thoroughly. Bring 3 liters of water to a boil in a large pot. Add the spinach and blanch for 3-4 minutes until wilted. Drain and immediately place in ice-cold water to stop cooking and retain the bright green color. Drain again.

STEP 2: Roughly chop the blanched spinach and add to a blender. Add 1/2 cup whole milk, 4 cloves garlic (peeled), 1 inch ginger (peeled), and 2-3 green chilies. Blend until completely smooth.

STEP 3: Pass the spinach mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any fibers, pressing gently with the back of a ladle. This gives a silky-smooth puree.

STEP 4: Cut 400g paneer into 1-inch cubes. Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a skillet and shallow-fry the paneer pieces until light golden on all sides, about 3-4 minutes total. Remove and set aside.

STEP 5: In the same skillet, add 1 tbsp ghee and 1 tsp cumin seeds. Let them crackle for 30 seconds. Add 1 inch ginger (minced) and 3 cloves garlic (minced). Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 6: Add 1 medium finely diced onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 4-5 minutes.

STEP 7: Add 1 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 1 minute. Then add the strained spinach puree and stir well.

STEP 8: Cook the spinach on medium heat for 5-6 minutes, stirring frequently, until it thickens slightly and the raw spinach smell disappears.

STEP 9: Add 1 cup heavy cream or condensed milk, 1/2 cup water (or more for desired consistency), salt to taste, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/4 tsp cumin powder, a pinch of nutmeg powder, and 1/4 tsp red chili powder.

STEP 10: Stir well and simmer for 3-4 minutes on low heat, allowing the flavors to meld. The gravy should be creamy and coat the back of a spoon.

STEP 11: Gently add the fried paneer pieces to the palak gravy. Stir carefully to avoid breaking the paneer.

STEP 12: Simmer for 2 minutes. Taste and adjust salt and spices. Add a squeeze of lemon juice for brightness if desired.

STEP 13: Finish with 1 tbsp butter and garnish with a spoon of fresh cream and 1 tbsp chopped cilantro.

STEP 14: Serve hot with steamed basmati rice, freshly made naan, or Indian bread. Accompany with sliced onion, lemon wedges, and pickle.
customIng: Ginger - 2 inch pieces (1 for blending, 1 minced)

customRecipes.strInstructions:
STEP 1: Cut 1 whole chicken (1.2 kg) into 8 pieces (legs, thighs, breasts, wings). Remove the skin if you prefer less fat. Make 3-4 diagonal cuts on each piece to allow the marinade to penetrate.

STEP 2: In a large mixing bowl, combine 1.5 cups plain yogurt (preferably Greek yogurt for tanginess), 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp White vinegar (optional, for extra tang), 2 tsp red chili powder, 1.5 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color), 1.5 tsp garam masala powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp black peppercorn powder, 1/4 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp oil.

STEP 3: Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and mix until each piece is well coated. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 6 hours (overnight is best for maximum flavor).

STEP 4: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C) or, if using a tandoor oven, prepare it for cooking.

STEP 5: If using an oven: Line a baking tray with aluminum foil, then place a wire rack on top. Brush the rack with oil. Arrange marinated chicken pieces on the rack in a single layer.

STEP 6: Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes at 450°F. The chicken should be cooked through with the internal temperature reaching 165°F (74°C).

STEP 7: For extra charring (tandoori effect), turn on the broiler for the last 3-4 minutes. Watch carefully to prevent burning.

STEP 8: If using a cast iron skillet: Heat a heavy skillet on high heat until smoking hot. Place marinated chicken pieces and cook for 6-8 minutes on the first side until they develop a nice sear and char marks. Flip and cook for another 6-8 minutes on the other side until cooked through.

STEP 9: Remove from heat. Brush the hot chicken with melted butter or ghee (1 tbsp). This gives a beautiful gloss and adds authentic flavor.

STEP 10: Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the chicken and garnish with finely chopped cilantro and mint.

STEP 11: Serve immediately on a bed of shredded onion with lemon wedges, sliced tomatoes, and cucumber slices. Serve with mint chutney or green chutney on the side. Can also be served as a main course with rice or bread.

STEP 1: Wash 1/2 cup basmati rice under running water until the water runs clear. Soak in water for 20 minutes, then drain.

STEP 2: Heat 1 liter whole milk in a heavy-bottomed saucepan on high heat. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.

STEP 3: Add the drained rice to the boiling milk and stir well. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, stirring frequently to prevent sticking and burning at the bottom.

STEP 4: Continue cooking for 20-25 minutes until the rice is completely soft and the milk has reduced to about 3/4 of the original volume. The texture should be creamy, with tender rice grains suspended throughout.

STEP 5: Add 1/2 cup sweetened condensed milk (or 1/4 cup powdered sugar) for sweetness. Stir well to combine. You can adjust sweetness to your preference.

STEP 6: Add flavoring: 1/4 tsp cardamom powder, a generous pinch of saffron strands (soaked in 2 tbsp milk), 1/4 tsp rose water, and 1/8 tsp nutmeg powder.

STEP 7: Stir in 1 tbsp ghee and 1 tbsp sliced almonds (fried in a bit of ghee). Reserve some nuts for garnish.

STEP 8: Simmer for another 2-3 minutes to let the flavors meld. The kheer should have a creamy, pudding-like consistency.

STEP 9: Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature. You can serve kheer warm, at room temperature, or chilled (refrigerate for at least 2 hours).

STEP 10: Transfer to serving bowls or a large serving dish. Garnish generously with sliced almonds, cashew pieces (fried), sliced pistachios, raisins, and a sprinkle of cardamom powder. Top with a dollop of whipped cream if desired.

STEP 1: Cut 4 medium potatoes and 1 small head of cauliflower into 1-inch cubes. Ensure pieces are uniform in size for even cooking.

STEP 2: Heat 4 tbsp oil or ghee in a large skillet or wok on high heat. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds and let them crackle for 30 seconds.

STEP 3: Add 1 inch ginger (minced) and 3 green chilies (slit lengthwise and deseeded if you prefer less heat). Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 4: Add 1 medium finely diced onion and cook until soft and beginning to turn golden, about 4-5 minutes.

STEP 5: Add the potato and cauliflower pieces and stir-fry on high heat for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally. The vegetables should start to soften.

STEP 6: Add 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt to taste. Mix thoroughly.

STEP 7: Add 1/2 cup water, lower heat to medium, cover with a lid, and cook for 10-12 minutes until the potatoes are cooked through and the cauliflower is tender but still has some bite. Stir once halfway through.

STEP 8: If there's excess liquid, increase heat and cook uncovered for the last 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are dry and the oil separates.

STEP 9: Taste and adjust salt and spices. Add 1 tbsp lemon juice for brightness.

STEP 10: Garnish with 2 tbsp finely chopped cilantro. Finish with 1 tbsp ghee if desired.

STEP 11: Serve hot as a side dish with rice or Indian bread. Also delicious with yogurt.

STEP 1: In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, bring 1 liter whole milk to a boil over high heat, stirring occasionally.

STEP 2: Once the milk comes to a rolling boil, add 1/4 cup lemon juice or white vinegar slowly while stirring. The milk will curdle immediately.

STEP 3: Remove from heat and let sit for 2-3 minutes to allow curds to fully separate from whey.

STEP 4: Line a fine-mesh sieve with cheesecloth. Pour the curdled milk through the sieve to drain out all the whey. Save the whey for cooking the rasgulla. Keep the ricotta-like paneer in the cloth.

STEP 5: Tie up the cheesecloth and hang it for 30 minutes to 1 hour to drain all excess moisture. The longer you hang, the drier the paneer.

STEP 6: Transfer the drained paneer to a large mixing bowl. Knead it gently with your palms for 5-8 minutes to make it smooth and soft. Do not over-knead, which will make it grainy.

STEP 7: Add 1/4 tsp cardamom powder (very fine) and, if desired, 1/4 tsp nutmeg powder to the kneaded paneer. Mix gently.

STEP 8: Measure 1 tbsp of the paneer mixture and roll between your palm and fingers to form a smooth, crack-free ball. If cracks appear, knead more. Repeat until all paneer is used—you should get 16-20 balls.

STEP 9: In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, add the reserved whey (should be about 3 cups) and bring to a simmer.

STEP 10: To the simmering whey, add 2 cups brown sugar or white sugar. Stir until sugar dissolves. Add 1/2 tsp cardamom powder, 1/4 tsp saffron strands, and 1/4 tsp rose water.

STEP 11: Gently slide the paneer balls into the simmering sugar syrup. Bring back to a simmer and cook for 8-10 minutes. The rasgulla will initially sink, then float to the surface after 2-3 minutes. They are done when they are spongy and double in size.

STEP 12: Remove from heat and let the rasgulla cool in the syrup to room temperature. Once cooled, refrigerate for at least 2-3 hours, preferably overnight. They continues to absorb syrup and become more soft and sweet as they sit.
customIng: Lemon Juice or White Vinegar - 1/4 cup
Brown Sugar or White Sugar - 2 cups

customRecipes.strInstructions:
STEP 1: Soak 1 cup black lentils (urad dal) and 1/4 cup kidney beans (rajma) together in water for 6-8 hours (or overnight). This helps them cook evenly and quickly.

STEP 2: Drain the soaked lentils and beans. In a large heavy-bottomed pot or pressure cooker, add the drained lentils and beans, 4 cups water, 1 tsp salt, 2 cloves minced garlic, and 1 tsp ginger (minced).

STEP 3: If using a pressure cooker: Cook for 8-10 whistles on high heat until the lentils and beans are completely soft and start breaking down. If using a pot: Boil for 45-60 minutes until very soft. There should be some liquid left; the mixture should be like a thick soup, not a paste.

STEP 4: Once cooked, mash some of the lentils against the side of the pot using the back of a spoon to create a creamy texture. Do not puree completely; some whole grains should remain.

STEP 5: In a separate large skillet, heat 4 tbsp butter on medium heat. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds and let them crackle for 30 seconds.

STEP 6: Add 1 medium finely diced onion and cook until deep golden brown, about 8-10 minutes, stirring frequently.

STEP 7: Add 2 tbsp ginger (minced), 4 cloves garlic (minced), and 2 green chilies (finely chopped). Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

STEP 8: Add 2 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 1 minute. Then add 1 cup crushed tomatoes and cook on high heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil separates from the sides.

STEP 9: Add 1.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, salt to taste, and 1/2 tsp sugar.

STEP 10: Cook this masala on medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently. The spices should become fragrant and slightly darker.

STEP 11: Add this masala to the cooked lentils and beans. Stir well to combine. If the mixture is too thick, add 1/2 to 1 cup more water as needed. It should be of pourable consistency.

STEP 12: Bring to a simmer and cook on low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally. The dal should have a rich, creamy, and thick consistency.

STEP 13: Stir in 1 cup heavy cream and 2 tbsp butter. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes. Taste and adjust salt and spices.

STEP 14: Garnish with 2 tbsp fresh cilantro, 1/2 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), a pinch of garam masala, and a drizzle of cream or butter on top.

STEP 15: Serve hot with basmati rice, naan, or roti. Pairs exceptionally well with tandoori chicken.

STEP 1: In a large mixing bowl, combine 3 cups all-purpose flour, 1 tsp instant yeast, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tbsp oil, and 3/4 tsp baking powder.

STEP 2: Add 3/4 cup warm whole milk gradually and mix until a shaggy dough forms. You may need to adjust milk quantity slightly—the dough should be soft but not sticky.

STEP 3: Knead the dough on a clean, lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. It should be soft and slightly sticky to the touch.

STEP 4: Place the kneaded dough in a lightly oiled bowl. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 4-6 hours until it doubles in size.

STEP 5: Punch down the dough to release the gas. Divide into 6-8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball.

STEP 6: Cover the dough balls with a damp cloth and let them rest for 30 minutes before rolling.

STEP 7: Take one dough ball and flatten slightly. Using a rolling pin, roll it into an ovoid (teardrop) shape, about 1/4 inch thick. The naan should be about 7-8 inches long.

STEP 8: Heat a cast iron skillet or tawa on high heat until very hot. (Ideally naan is made in a tandoor oven, but a skillet works well.)

STEP 9: Carefully place one naan on the hot skillet. Cook for 2-3 minutes without moving it. Bubbles will form on the surface.

STEP 10: Flip the naan and cook the other side for 1-2 minutes until golden brown spots appear. Some charring is desirable for authentic tandoori effect.

STEP 11: Brush the hot naan with melted butter or ghee. Brush both sides.

STEP 12: Transfer to a plate. Optionally press a mixture of minced garlic and cilantro onto the butter while still hot, making Garlic Naan.

STEP 13: Stack the hot naan and wrap in a clean cloth to keep them soft and warm. Repeat with remaining dough.

STEP 14: Serve hot, ideally within minutes of cooking. Naan can be served with curries, as a starter with dips, or alongside tandoori preparations.
